steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The relative clause whose body of ______ requires a subject and a finite verb. The text following the blank begins with the adjective interdisciplinary, which needs a verb to establish itself as a predicate.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause rule
steps.content:
A relative clause must have a finite verb as its main predicate. B's work is provides this: whose body of work is interdisciplinary creates a complete relative clause with is as the finite verb and interdisciplinary as the predicate adjective. A, C, and D offer only non-finite structures (participial “having been,” infinitive “to be,” and participle “being”) that cannot serve as the main predicate of the clause.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
